join_order.notes: Probe pre_by_adsh_tag_ver bucket hash index; no match = passes anti-join. Use MADV_RANDOM on mmap.

scan.access_pattern: sequential within qualifying blocks, two-phase: filter then probe+aggregate

data_structures.uom_dict: load dictionary to find USD code
data_structures.aggregation: flat_open_addressing_hash_map<uint64_t, {int64_t cnt, double total}>, power-of-2 slots, linear probing
data_structures.selection_vector: thread-local uint32_t[] of qualifying row indices per block

parallelism.aggregation_strategy: thread-local flat hash maps (open-addressing, 128K slots each ~3MB, fits in LLC), merge sequentially after scan
parallelism.merge_optimization: merge thread-local maps into global flat map; skip empty slots during merge via sentinel key

indexes.purpose: restrict scan to USD rows only

optimization_notes: Category B+D: Two root causes identified. (1) std::unordered_map uses chained hashing with node-based allocation causing severe cache misses during both scan-time insertion and the merge phase (sort_topk=9ms). Replace with flat open-addressing hash map using power-of-2 sizing (128K slots × 24 bytes = 3MB per thread, fits in LLC). Use sentinel key UINT64_MAX for empty slots; linear probing. (2) Two-phase scan within each morsel: first pass filters ddate+value sequentially to build a compact selection vector of qualifying row indices, second pass loads sub_fk/tag_code/version_code only for qualifying rows and does index probe + aggregation. This improves branch prediction and reduces column reads for the ~73% of rows eliminated by ddate+value filters. For the anti-join probe, pack the 3-field comparison by checking a combined 64-bit value first (sub_fk<<32|tag_code) before checking version_code to reduce comparison cost. Merge phase: iterate flat map slots skipping sentinels (sequential scan, no pointer chasing). Expected improvement: ~5ms from flat hash map in scan, ~4ms from faster merge = target ~63ms.
